topic: How to value a used crane before selling
primary_keyword: how to value a used crane

seo_strategy.search_intent: Informational with commercial investigation intent; the reader wants a practical method to estimate used crane value before deciding whether to sell privately or through auction, and is likely evaluating professional support.
seo_strategy.opportunity_rationale: High seller usefulness and good pre-listing fit, but slightly harder than more explicit transaction-adjacent auction topics.
recommended_internal_link_types: current and recent crane auction listings

brief.summary: Create a practical, seller-focused guide that explains how to value a used crane before selling, with clear steps for estimating used crane value, identifying crane appraisal factors, and avoiding common pricing mistakes. The article should help crane owners, fleet managers, dealers, and contractors understand what affects crane resale value, while positioning Jeff Martin Auctioneers as the trusted advisory and auction partner for getting the best outcome. Include a simple equipment valuation checklist, mention when a professional auction assessment makes sense, and use strong CTAs that point readers to Jeff Martin Auctioneers for valuation support, auction listings, and contact options.
outline: H1: How to Value a Used Crane Before Selling
H2: Start with the crane’s basic details: make, model, year, and configuration
H2: The main crane appraisal factors that influence used crane value
H2: Build an equipment valuation checklist before you price the machine
H2: Compare market demand, recent auction results, and replacement cost
H2: Common mistakes sellers make when estimating what affects crane resale value
H2: When to get a professional valuation or auction advisory opinion from Jeff Martin Auctioneers
H2: Next steps: prepare your crane for sale and request a market-based evaluation
